mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate and supports equity multiples, so when the policy rate is falling the S&P 500 should post positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is rejected if rate cuts do not coincide with higher short-horizon SPX returns.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the following month. The effect is strongest when unemployment is accelerating rather than merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS indicates resilient nominal demand and revenue momentum, which should support SPX performance over the next 5 trading days through earnings sentiment and cyclicals leadership.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month RSAFS surprises do not correspond to positive 5-day SPX returns at a hit rate above 52% over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS rise together, firms are expanding output and hiring simultaneously, confirming real activity breadth that should lift equity prices over the next quarter. The effect depends on joint improvement rather than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-signal portfolio does not outperform single-proxy rules, or if positive INDPRO-and-PAYEMS months fail to produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: High CPIAUCSL inflation combined with a low FEDFUNDS policy rate implies negative real rates and an accommodative financial condition for risk assets, so SPX should rise over the next 20 trading days until the policy stance tightens. The signal fails once real-rate pressure becomes obvious.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with CPIAUCSL inflation above FEDFUNDS do not show positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the spread loses predictive power after real rates turn positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals housing demand deterioration and weaker future consumption, which should translate into negative SPX returns over the next month as cyclical earnings expectations are revised down. The mechanism is strongest during broad demand slowdowns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases or if the average return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in UNRATE and slowdown in PAYEMS indicates labor-market deterioration that should lead equity investors to price recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal requires both weaker employment growth and higher unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if combined UNRATE increases and PAYEMS contractions do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in a majority of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a soft-landing manufacturing rebound, rising INDPRO after a prior slowdown signals improving production and margin normalization, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is not expected to work during inflationary supply shocks.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 6-month moving average after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if recovery-triggered INDPRO upturns fail to deliver positive 60-day SPX returns above baseline, or if the hit rate stays below 53% across walk-forward tests.
